entry.title: SAP’s Latest AI Updates: What Every HR Leader Needs to Know in 2025
entry.content:
Introduction

AI isn’t just a thing of the future - it’s here, transforming how businesses operate, especially in Human Resources (HR). SAP is leading the race with AI-powered upgrades to its HR solutions within the SAP SuccessFactors Human Capital Management (HCM) suite. These innovations are designed to make HR professionals jobs easier, improve employee experiences, and create more agile workplaces. Let’s deep dive into how AI is reshaping HR and the latest game-changing announcements from SAP.

AI-Driven Enhancements in SAP SuccessFactors

  • AI-Assisted Writing: Writing performance goals, feedback, and other HR-related documents can be time-consuming. SAP’s AI-powered writing assistant helps craft professional and effective communications, cutting down the time spent on these tasks.

  • AI-Assisted Person Insights: Preparing for one-on-one meetings or performance reviews? This tool gives managers a quick yet detailed summary of employees’ job histories, compensation data, and key achievements - saving time while ensuring more meaningful discussions.
  • AI-Assisted Career Insights: Employees no longer need to navigate career growth on their own. AI provides personalised recommendations for skills development, training courses, and mentorship opportunities, improving engagement and speeding up skill acquisition.
  • AI-Assisted Job Description Enhancement: Hiring the right talent starts with writing the right job description. AI helps recruiters and hiring managers fine-tune job postings to make them more engaging, inclusive, and aligned with business needs.

  • AI-Assisted Skills Architecture Creation: Keeping track of employee skills can be challenging. AI automates the creation of a comprehensive skills library by analyzing job descriptions, ensuring organizations stay up-to-date with evolving talent requirements.
  • AI-Assisted Skills Standardization: Different teams may define the same skills differently. AI helps standardize skills across the organization, bringing consistency and clarity to competency frameworks.
  • AI-Assisted Microsoft Teams App Chat: Employees can now interact with SAP SuccessFactors within Microsoft Teams using natural language, making HR processes smoother and more accessible.

Announcements, Strategic Partnerships and Ecosystem Expansion

  • Unveiling SAP Joule Agents: February 2025, SAP introduced Joule Agents, AI-powered assistants designed to automate complex, cross-functional workflows across finance, service, and sales, significantly improving business productivity. Powered by SAP’s Business Data Cloud, these agents access unified, context-rich data from SAP and non-SAP sources, enabling advanced reasoning and problem-solving. Additionally, SAP Knowledge Graph enhances their decision-making by revealing critical connections between data and processes, reinforcing SAP’s commitment to AI-driven operational efficiency and seamless collaboration. Be ready to build your agents using Joule Studio. 

 

Pic Courtesy:SAP

 

  • Collaboration with Databricks: SAP Business Data Cloud, announced on 13th February 2025, is a game-changer. By partnering with Databricks, SAP is enabling businesses to seamlessly unify, govern, and analyze SAP and third-party data, unlocking new AI possibilities.

 Pic Courtesy:SAP

 

 

  • Integration with WalkMe: To make HR software more intuitive, SAP plans to integrate pre-built WalkMe content into SuccessFactors. This feature, set for release in the 1H of 2025, will improve employee adoption and streamline digital workflows. Surely a game changer!

 

Recognition and Future Outlook

SAP’s leadership in AI-driven HR solutions has not gone unnoticed:

  • Leader in Fosway 9-Grid™ for Cloud HR: In September 2024, SAP was named a Strategic Leader in the 2024 Fosway 9-Grid for Cloud HR, reinforcing its dominance in the market with a comprehensive, forward-thinking HR suite.

As businesses embrace AI-driven HR tools, SAP continues to push boundaries, ensuring that AI remains ethical, efficient, and empowering for employees and HR teams alike. The future of work is here and SAP is helping organizations make the most of it.

Note: The above blog is based on latest announcements & features (as of 16th February, 2025). For the latest updates, please refer to SAP’s official communications.

Looking forward to catching up with you soon with a new blog!

entry.title: Re: SAP SuccessFactors Compensation Introduction & Overview - Part 2
entry.content: @KarlaFalconi  I have the same business scenarios. May I know you figured it out?

entry.title: The Catalog Item created in S/4 Hana using “Manage Catalog Item” under not available for selection
entry.content:
Issue : The Catalog Item created in S/4 Hana using “Manage Catalog Item” under Procurement Internal Catalog (S4H_GLOBALCATALOG) is active and can be searched but it is  not available for selection in Cross-Catalog Search during Purchase requisition creation using My Purchase Requisition. 

The required configurations to allow the catalog to be visible is done.

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP : Error in SAP Build Apps
entry.content:
Hi there, the issue is still there, Subscription to SAP Build Apps is not working either from the booster, or from the manual setup. All the time it fails.

On my second trial account, where the Service SAP Build Apps was configured few weeks before, there is now an issue with access to a project - Error msg 403, info msg Access denied, You don't have authorization to view this page.

Please support, it seems to be a global issue with SAP Build Apps and SAP BTP trial account.

@Dan_Wroblewski

entry.title: RAP On-Stack Developer Extensibility
entry.content:
Problem statement:
How to develop custom extensions for standard ABAP RAP based business objects in S/4HANA Public, Private Cloud, BTP ABAP Environment and On-Premise Platforms?

Solution:
RAP Developer Extensibility is the solution. The topic gives an overview of RAP developer extensibility architecture, extension development artifacts such as BO interfaces, all available extensibility features and how to develop RAP custom extensions such as data model extension, behavior extensions and node extensions for standard ABAP RAP based applications.

I am on of the presenter for this topic at ABAP Conference and int the below youtube recording. This topic covers contented related to extensibility architecture overview, extensibility use cases,  RAP BO Interfaces Concept, How Procurement Project Standard RAP based application is extended with custom extensions and some useful links which can be explored related to RAP Developer Extensibility. 

ABAP Conference recording:  Deep Dive into RAP Developer Extensibility

Slides: https://abapconf.org/abapconfeurope2024/w1w2.html

Check for topic "Deep Dive into RAP Developer Extensibility" where you can find the link to download slides in pdf document.

Use cases covered:

  1. Data Model Extensions 
    • Adding Custom fields to standard CDS views
  2. Behavior and Field related behavior extensions 
    • Adding Dynamic feature control for custom field based on standard field.
    • Adding a new Custom Action
    • Adding Validation for standard field.

  3. Node Extensions
    • Adding a new custom node for a standard RAP BO.
  4. Service Definition Extension

Note:  Demo shown in youtube recording for procurement project application is implemented without using wizards. That means all required extension objects are created manually in ADT.

In ABAP Cloud in the latest version you can also use below ABAP Repository object wizard for generating extension fields to the selected  RAP Business Object.

Below TechEd git code sample exercises give more information about developer extensibility generation using above wizard 
RAP630 - Use ABAP Cloud for developer extensibility where Developer Extensibility is used.
Exercise 3 - Extend the CDS data model using the field extension wizard

You can find out all released RAP BO Interfaces in SAP Business Accelerator Hub in the On stack Extensibility section.

entry.title: Re: SAP Build process automation- Access Denied
entry.content: I had same issue but it now works after I did your steps, thanks

entry.title: Re: Issue when deploying simple app to BTP using 'cf push'
entry.content:
Hi,
Using 'CF push' is not the recommended approach for deploying SAP Fiori applications to SAP BTP, Cloud Foundry environment.
Please refer for the following documentation on how to generate CF deployment configuration for your application in SAP Business Application Studio, and later use it to deploy your application:
https://help.sap.com/docs/bas/developing-sap-fiori-app-in-sap-business-application-studio/generate-deployment-configuration-cloud-foundry

https://help.sap.com/docs/bas/developing-sap-fiori-app-in-sap-business-application-studio/deployment-of-application#deployment-to-cloud-foundry

You can also use SAP Business Application Studio task explorer to easily create and run the relevant tasks to create this configuration and use it for the deployment.

Thanks,

Rotem

entry.title: Re: IPS Target system data is not reading from IAS source...
entry.content:
@Venkat53 

Please check the below help documentation which will provide you hint for your issue..
https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-identity-services/cloud-identity-services/target-sap-btp-xs-advanced-uaa-cloud-foundry

Kindly add screenshot for any issues you can encounter..
I'm assuming , pls check the code and update the right properties.  
Source System : SAP Cloud Identity Services IAS

Target System : SAP BTP XS Advanced UAA (Cloud foundry)

entry.title: Re: Issue when deploying simple app to BTP using 'cf push'
entry.content: Editing here - that if your target goal is to deploy to SAP S/4HANA Cloud, you should create deployment configuration to ABAP system and not to deploy to CF at all which is completely different runtime environment. See: https://help.sap.com/docs/bas/developing-sap-fiori-app-in-sap-business-application-studio/generate-deployment-configuration-abap

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP : Error in SAP Build Apps
entry.content: I am not in dev or the product team, but already last week I alerted the dev team to posts like yours where people could not start the service. With your post, I asked for an update. I'm sure they are working hard on this, and hopefully soon it will be fixed.

entry.title: Re: SAP Business Application Studio?
entry.content: Thanks @JansiMurugesan for your valuable inputs. Now I am clear.

entry.title: Re: Watch the Replays of Our Exclusive SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 Early Release Series!
entry.content:
Hi,

Why there is no tag for asset management? As tags are a big advantages to find this articles from tons of blogs, correct tags help very well.

BR,

Adventurer

entry.title: Dynamic Stock Calculation in SAP Datasphere: Handling Non-Cumulative Stock Movements | S/4 IM
entry.content:
Ever wondered how to set up a dynamic solution for inventory snapshots—whether you’re looking at cumulative or non-cumulative stock? Curious about how this is done in S/4HANA’s VDM? If so, you’ve landed on the right post.

Let’s talk about what you need from three different example sources:

  • ECC:

    • You can grab your initial stock from MARD (the BX extractor).
    • MSEG is where you’ll find your delta movements (BF extractor).

  • BW Inbound Layer:

    • Your stock movements should be in an inbound ADSO before heading to the non-cumulative cube.
    • Missing the initial snapshot? No worries—just filter a BW cube query by date, publish it to HANA, where it will use the L-script into a Calculation View that SAP Datasphere can read.

  • S/4HANA:

    • Both initial stock and delta movements are in MATDOC.
    • There’s a harmonization layer that keeps track of two views:
      • Initial Stock State (loaded once and stays put)
      • Delta Movements (continually grows)

Once these views are in place, you can generate daily stock numbers—both cumulative and non-cumulative - for any time frame you want.

To achieve this, the S/4 logic of the Projection View - P_MaterialStockTimeSeries1 comes as an inspiration. It is the core view calculating the stock in S/4 HANA for any of the upper applications. It consists of three elements:

  1. I_MaterialStockPeriodsSingle
    1. Provides a range of dates, so that they can be joined to. Imagine this is just a table with a few dates, so if you specify the dates below, you will get the result as a table with all the days in between the provided range. The granularity can be changed by using a PERIODTYPE parameter. It accepts Quarters, Years, and Weeks. 

 

SELECT 
   I_MATERIALSTOCKPERIODSSINGLE~PERIODTYPE, 
   I_MATERIALSTOCKPERIODSSINGLE~FISCALYEARVARIANT, 
   I_MATERIALSTOCKPERIODSSINGLE~STARTDATE, 
   I_MATERIALSTOCKPERIODSSINGLE~ENDDATE, 
   I_MATERIALSTOCKPERIODSSINGLE~YEARPERIOD
 FROM 
  I_MATERIALSTOCKPERIODSSINGLE( P_STARTDATE = '20210610' , 
   P_ENDDATE = '20211010', 
   P_PERIODTYPE = 'D' )​

 

  • P_MaterialStock_Aggr
    1. It provides an aggregated view of the stock, representing the issued and received stock (delta running) at a given time range. It takes all the changes to the material from the start to the end date.
      DateChangeAggregated Change Output
      01.01.2021(+) 33
      02.01.2021(-) 5-2
      03.01.2021(+) 108
  • P_MaterialStockByKeyDate1
    1. This view provides an opening balance for the first date of the range provided by the user. It aggregates the initial stock and all the movements until the one specified by the start date. Then this value is repeated over all the dates within the range specified by the user. 
      DateStock Value at 01.01.2021
      01.01.2021221
      02.01.2021221
      03.01.2021221

When those three components are used together in the code below, you can expect the following result:

DateChangeAggregated Change OutputStock Value at 01.01.2021Result
01.01.2021(+) 33221224
02.01.2021(-) 5-2221219
03.01.2021(+) 108221229

 

@AbapCatalog.preserveKey: true
@AbapCatalog.sqlViewName: 'PMATSTCKTIMESER1'

@AbapCatalog.compiler.compareFilter: true
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck:#NOT_REQUIRED
@ObjectModel.usageType.sizeCategory: #XXL
@ObjectModel.usageType.serviceQuality: #D
@ObjectModel.usageType.dataClass:#TRANSACTIONAL
@ClientHandling.algorithm: #SESSION_VARIABLE

@VDM.private: true
@VDM.viewType: #COMPOSITE

// The client field in the two JOIN conditions below is used as a pseudo condition
// because there is no real common field in both JOINed views
// but fields from both views are required in each result row
// (client can be used even if it is not declared in the DDL of the views itself but the generated SE11 view has a client field)


// Having the fields for PeriodType and PostingDates in the ON condition of the JOIN with the type and date parameter
// is quite faster than having the PeriodType and PostingDates in the WHERE clause

define view P_MaterialStockTimeSeries1
  with parameters
    P_StartDate  : vdm_v_start_date,
    P_EndDate    : vdm_v_end_date,
    P_PeriodType : nsdm_period_type
  as select from    I_MaterialStockPeriodsSingle(P_StartDate:  :P_StartDate,
                 P_EndDate:    :P_EndDate,
                 P_PeriodType: :P_PeriodType ) as a
    left outer join P_MaterialStock_Aggr       as b on  a.mandt                  =  b.mandt
                                                    and a.PeriodType             =  :P_PeriodType
                                                    and b.MatlDocLatestPostgDate >= :P_StartDate
                                                    and b.MatlDocLatestPostgDate <= a.EndDate
{
      // Period
  key a.PeriodType,
  key a.StartDate,
  key a.EndDate,
  key a.YearPeriod,
      // Stock Identifier
  key b.Material,
  key b.Plant,
  key b.StorageLocation,
  key b.Batch,
  key b.Supplier,
  key b.SDDocument,
  key b.SDDocumentItem,
  key b.WBSElementInternalID,
  key b.Customer,
  key b.InventoryStockType,
  key b.InventorySpecialStockType,
  key b.CostEstimate,    
      // Units
  key b.MaterialBaseUnit,
      // Stock Groups
  key b.CompanyCode,
  key a.FiscalYearVariant,
  key b.Currency,
      // Quantity and Value
      @Semantics.quantity.unitOfMeasure: 'MaterialBaseUnit'
      sum(b.MatlWrhsStkQtyInMatlBaseUnit) as MatlWrhsStkQtyInMatlBaseUnit,
      
            //Consumption
      @Semantics.quantity.unitOfMeasure: 'MaterialBaseUnit'
      sum( case when MatlDocLatestPostgDate < StartDate 
        then
         0
        else
         MatlCnsmpnQtyInMatlBaseUnit
        end   
      ) as MatlCnsmpnQtyInMatlBaseUnit,
      
      //Sum for Average
      @Semantics.quantity.unitOfMeasure: 'MaterialBaseUnit'
      cast(sum( 
        case when MatlDocLatestPostgDate < StartDate 
        then
         cast( b.MatlWrhsStkQtyInMatlBaseUnit as abap.dec(25,3)) *  (dats_days_between(StartDate, EndDate)+1) 
        else
         cast( b.MatlWrhsStkQtyInMatlBaseUnit as abap.dec(25,3)) *  (dats_days_between(MatlDocLatestPostgDate, EndDate)+1) 
        end   
      ) as abap.dec(25,3)) as MatlStkQtySumInPeriod
}
group by
  a.PeriodType,
  a.StartDate,
  a.EndDate,
  a.YearPeriod,
  b.Material,
  b.Plant,
  b.StorageLocation,
  b.Batch,
  b.Supplier,
  b.SDDocument,
  b.SDDocumentItem,
  b.WBSElementInternalID,
  b.Customer,
  b.InventoryStockType,
  b.InventorySpecialStockType,
  b.CostEstimate,
  b.MaterialBaseUnit,
  b.CompanyCode,
  a.FiscalYearVariant,
  b.Currency

union all

// Total Stock before StartDate Parameter
select from       I_MaterialStockPeriodsSingle(P_StartDate:  :P_StartDate,
            P_EndDate:    :P_EndDate,
            P_PeriodType: :P_PeriodType )                             as a
  left outer join P_MaterialStockByKeyDate1(P_KeyDate:  :P_StartDate) as b on  a.mandt      = b.mandt
                                                                           and a.PeriodType = :P_PeriodType
{
      // Period
  key a.PeriodType,
  key a.StartDate,
  key a.EndDate,
  key a.YearPeriod,
      // Stock Identifier
  key b.Material,
  key b.Plant,
  key b.StorageLocation,
  key b.Batch,
  key b.Supplier,
  key b.SDDocument,
  key b.SDDocumentItem,
  key b.WBSElementInternalID,
  key b.Customer,
  key b.InventoryStockType,
  key b.InventorySpecialStockType,
  key b.CostEstimate,
      // Units
  key b.MaterialBaseUnit,
      // Stock Groups
  key b.CompanyCode,
  key a.FiscalYearVariant,
  key b.Currency,
      // Values
      @Semantics.quantity.unitOfMeasure: 'MaterialBaseUnit'
      sum(b.MatlWrhsStkQtyInMatlBaseUnit)   as MatlWrhsStkQtyInMatlBaseUnit, // there is only one row per Date
      0 as MatlCnsmpnQtyInMatlBaseUnit,
      cast( MatlWrhsStkQtyInMatlBaseUnit as abap.dec(25,3)) *  (dats_days_between(StartDate, EndDate)+1) as MatlStkQtySumInPeriod
}
group by
  a.PeriodType,
  a.StartDate,
  a.EndDate,
  a.YearPeriod,
  b.Material,
  b.Plant,
  b.StorageLocation,
  b.Batch,
  b.Supplier,
  b.SDDocument,
  b.SDDocumentItem,
  b.WBSElementInternalID,
  b.Customer,
  b.InventoryStockType,
  b.InventorySpecialStockType,
  b.CostEstimate,
  b.MaterialBaseUnit,
  b.CompanyCode,
  a.FiscalYearVariant,
  b.Currency,
  MatlWrhsStkQtyInMatlBaseUnit

 

Now, to apply the same solution in the DSP view, one will need the Initial Stock State, Delta movements, and A dynamic view providing the date ranges. An example of the view implementation for the range of the dates with Day granularity, in the same view other granularities should be added:

 

    select
        'D'                                           as PeriodType,
        ' '                                           as FiscalYearVariant,
        concat(cd.YEAR, DAYOFYEAR(cd.DATE_SQL))       as YearPeriod,
        min(cd.DATE_SQL)                              as StartDate,
        max(cd.DATE_SQL)                              as EndDate
    from  
        "SAP.TIME.M_TIME_DIMENSION" as cd    
    where
          cd.DATE_SQL >= :IP_DATEFROM
      and cd.DATE_SQL <= :IP_DATETO
    group by
      cd.YEAR,
      DAYOFYEAR(cd.DATE_SQL)

 

Once those views are available, the following code can be used to dynamically create the non-cumulative stock snapshot for any date range:

  1. Just like in I_MaterialStockPeriodsSingle, select the date range and put it in the internal table:

 

lt_time_ser = 
    SELECT
        "PeriodType",
        "FiscalYearVariant",
        "YearPeriod",
        "StartDate",
        "EndDate"
    FROM
        <...>"(
            IP_DATEFROM => :IP_DATEFROM,
            IP_DATETO => :IP_DATETO,
            IP_PERIODTYPE => :IP_PERIODTYPE,
            IP_FISCALVARIANT => :IP_FISCALVARIANT
        )​

 

  • Just like in P_MaterialStock_Aggr, select the movements:

 

lt_movements = 
    SELECT
        a."PeriodType",
        a."FiscalYearVariant",
        a."YearPeriod",
        a."StartDate",
        a."EndDate",
        b.MATERIAL,
        b.PLANT,
        SUM(b."QTY") as "...",
        SUM(b."VAL") as "...",
        ...
    FROM 
        :lt_time_series AS a
    LEFT JOIN 
        ViewWithMovementsAndInit AS b ON(
            a."PeriodType"          =  :IP_PERIODTYPE
            AND b.PostingDate       >= :IP_DATEFROM
            AND b.PostingDate       <= a."EndDate"
        )
    GROUP BY
      (...)
;​

 

  • Just like in P_MaterialStockByKeyDate1, select the initial stock at the beginning of the range for each of the dates:

 

lt_opening_balance = 
    SELECT
        a."PeriodType",
        a."FiscalYearVariant",
        a."YearPeriod",
        a."StartDate",
        a."EndDate",
        b.MATERIAL,
        b.PLANT,
    	SUM(b."QTY") as "QTY",
    	SUM(b."VAL") as "VAL",
        (...)
    FROM 
        :lt_time_series AS a
    LEFT JOIN 
        ViewWithMovementsAndInit(
            IP_KEYDATE => ADD_DAYS(:IP_DATEFROM,-1) 
        ) AS b ON( 
            a."PeriodType" = :IP_PERIODTYPE
        )
    GROUP BY
        (...)
;​

 

If you would like to add cumulative figures, such as issued and received stock, simply select them and add them with the union at the end. 

Now that the dynamic view is available, you can create a history by using data flows which will select the data from this dynamic view and add it to the partitioned historical table. Virtual views with snapshots can be created on top of this view to support the stock changes that happened retroactively in the last few months.

You might ask, why all this hassle if the analytical model already supports the non-cumulative key figures? https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_DATASPHERE/c8a54ee704e94e15926551293243fd1d/589908814adc4f16bb2e158794f9a96a.html Both for performance reasons and because the Analytical Model cannot be used as a data source like Query as an InfoProvider in BW or HANA view generated for a query with L script.

______________________________________________________________________________________

This blog explained how to build a dynamic stock snapshot by combining initial stock data with subsequent delta movements from various systems:

  • Data Sources:

    • ECC: Use tables like MARD (initial stock) and MSEG (delta movements).
    • BW Inbound Layer: Stock movements are stored in the inbound ADSO; if the initial snapshot is missing, it can be recreated via a date-filtered query on the BW cube and published to HANA.
    • S/4: Both the initial stock and subsequent changes are available in the MATDOC table, with a harmonization layer maintaining separate views for initial stock (static) and delta movements (growing).

  • S/4 Logic with Projection View:
    The core view P_MaterialStockTimeSeries1 is inspired by three elements:

    1. I_MaterialStockPeriodsSingle: Generates a continuous range of dates (with adjustable granularity).
    2. P_MaterialStock_Aggr: Aggregates issued and received stock over the period.
    3. P_MaterialStockByKeyDate1: Provides the opening balance as of the first date, repeated for the entire period.

  • Dynamic View for DSP:
    Similar logic is applied to create a dynamic view that:

    • Retrieves the date range,
    • Aggregates delta movements, and
    • Extracts the initial stock at the start of the range.
      This allows for generating non-cumulative snapshots and, if needed, adding cumulative figures through union operations.

  • Historical and Performance Considerations:
    The dynamic view can feed partitioned historical tables and virtual snapshot views, supporting retroactive stock changes. This approach is preferred over using the standard Analytical Model due to performance benefits and enhanced data source compatibility for BW or HANA query scenarios.

In essence, by harmonizing initial and delta stock data across systems and leveraging dynamic views, you can flexibly and efficiently compute daily stock snapshots for various reporting and analytical needs.

entry.title: Re: G/L Account field not found in Manage Project Demand...
entry.content:
Hi Mahmood, 

you have to assign the valuation class for the material group in CBC. Based on this assignment the system determines the accoun from the automatic postings setup. 

Regards

Juraj

entry.title: Performance Optimization in field symbols
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

There was an existing code as below where observed that it consumes some time in the trace and <lt_data> holds around 40 records.

Is there an option that i can fine tune this to increase the performance. Thanks.

        LOOP AT <lt_data> ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<fs_data>).
          ASSIGN COMPONENT 'fpm_bol_guibb_ref_0000000009' OF STRUCTURE <fs_data> TO FIELD-SYMBOL(<fs_value>).
          IF <fs_value> IS ASSIGNED.
            <fs_value> = abap_true.
          ENDIF.
        ENDLOOP.

 

REgards,

Karthik S

entry.title: Re: SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA – SAP Fiori Security Design Fundamentals
entry.content:
@Jocelyn_Dart- Thanks for your response on my query, it is good to see the composite role support has now been increased post S4HANA 2023 FPS 1, which will ease out the admin part.

However, I still have the question on business role delivered by SAP, whether they should be a single role or should represent a composite role i.e bundling different single roles ( in terms of task) from authorization design perspective?

I understand some of them are composite and some of them are single as you mentioned above but not sure why this arrangement?

If you can share your view that would be great, in my view business role must be composite roles, to simply the authorization design from both user assignment and role maintenance perspective?

Thanks,

Sameer.

entry.title: Re: Deployment of Organizational Structure from Developme...
entry.content: Hi arunacheemala7 , thanks for detail reply, how we will avoid the scope and organization structure created on development tenant to move to QA as the scope and organizational structure created on customizing tenant has already moved to QAS and is the final one.

entry.title: Registrations for SAP CHOTA TALKS 2025 Q1 (SAP Stammtisch Plus)
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community members,

You are cordially invited to the SAP Stammtisch Plus event happening in Hyderabad which will include an engaging &  insightful sessions on SAP various topics and networking.

Date: Saturday 22rd Feb, 2025
Time: 3:00 PM to 06:00 PM
Venue: University of Hyderabad
Prof CR Rao Road, Hyderabad Central University Rd, Gachibowli, Hyderabad, Telangana 500046
(Google Maps)

 

Please register here!! Limited seats only so hurry up (100 Participants only) 

Note: This is an In-person Event only

entry.title: Re: Fiori LaunchPad Mega-Tutorial
entry.content:
Hi Smith, Thanks for sharing the detailed, to the point info. I am looking for option where fiori app remain in-place when clicking on App application like URL but in our case its always opening in new tab. Any idea how to make it open in same tab?

Regards,

Vaibhav

entry.title: How to Change Schema Name to Lowercase in Full Stack Cloud Application
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I have developed a full stack cloud application and added the schema to it. However, the schema name is currently in camel case, and I want to convert it entirely to lowercase.

For example, it is currently displayed as VehicleInspection-db, but I would like to change it to vehicleinspection-db.

Here are the steps I have taken so far:

  1. I manually created the HDI Container and bound my application to it using the following command:

     
    cds bind -2 instancename:sharedkey

  2. However, when I execute the command cds deploy, it creates a new container with the name VehicleInspection-db again.

How can I achieve the desired lowercase schema name?

Thank you in advance for your help!

Best regards,

Rahul Jain

entry.title: Re: How to get Variant Management tile in Build Work Zone?
entry.content: hello did you find the solution? I am having the same problem too...

entry.title: Prompted for username and password when running Fiori App on VSCODE localhost on my mac
entry.content:
I am developing the example Fiori Travel application, where we create a list report with object view.  This was developed correctly using eclipse and when executing the OData service preview there were no issues.  The next stage was to run on vscode locally, which I did, followed all the steps using the application generator.  The problem is when I run the application and it opens in localhost, I get the below error, asking for username and password.  I have many other local web servers using local host on python and nodejs wihtout issue.  The issue is I think it is was trying to authenticate from the btp abap backend where my OData service is kept.  I tried setting a username and password for the btp destination, but still no success.  I also tried changing the auths on my mac for the webapp folders and all sub folders, still no luck.

Please advise.

Best
Sulman

 

This is my ui5.yml file:

entry.title: Re: Consume Ariba APIs using Postman
entry.content:
Great Blog. Thanks for sharing the input.

Is it a good practice to send the client ID and credentials in the Params tab, as they will appear in the URL? Instead, it is better to include the client credentials in the Body using x-www-form-urlencoded format.

entry.title: Re: Performance Optimization in field symbols
entry.content: "Some time", how much? Only 40 records? Probably it executes in 7 microseconds, but you could optimize it to 6 microseconds. So, it's not worth "optimizing". The only reason why it could take more time than 7 microseconds, is that the internal table used to contain millions of records which were deleted, in that case it might be worth optimizing (not even sure). Also, maybe you have incorrectly analyzed the performance issue.

entry.title: JS engine in XS hana classic
entry.content:
Does anyone knows what version of SpiderMonkey/ecma script hana XS classic uses? Is it different in different SPs of hana or the same in each system?
My intent was to transpile typescript into something that hana would understand, but not sure which compilation target should I choose

entry.title: SAP JIT/JIS – Efficient Just-in-Time and Just-in-Sequence Processes in Logistics
entry.content:
Just-in-Time (JIT) and Just-in-Sequence (JIS) processes play a crucial role in the modern manufacturing and automotive industries. They ensure optimized material supply, reduce storage costs and enable smooth production. SAP offers comprehensive support for these processes with its modules - but in practice it turns out that hardly any company uses a pure SAP standard solution. Instead, there are usually customer-specific adaptations that are similar in their basic principle

JIT and JIS – The fundamental difference

Just-in-Time (JIT) ensures that materials are delivered exactly when they are needed in production. This significantly reduces inventory levels and increases flexibility. Just-in-Sequence (JIS) goes a step further: Here, materials are not only delivered on time, but also in the order in which they must be used directly in production or assembly. This is particularly essential in the automotive industry, where specific components must be provided at exactly the right time and in the right order.

Just-in-Sequence (JIS) goes one step further: Here, the materials are not only delivered on time, but also in the order in which they must be used directly in production or assembly. This is particularly essential in the automotive industry, where specific components must be provided at exactly the right time and in the right order.

 

SAP JIT/JIS in Practice

SAP offers solutions for these processes in the area of ​​SAP S/4HANA Supply Chain Management , especially in the SAP JIT Inbound Processing module . However, the implementation approach varies depending on the customer:

  • Customer-specific EDI connections : Delivery schedules, detailed delivery schedules and ASN messages are often individually designed to meet the specific requirements of the company and its suppliers.
  • Tailor-made sequence controls : Many companies use their own logic for sequence planning because the SAP standard does not always cover all industry-specific requirements.
  • Integration with production systems : JIT/JIS processes must interact seamlessly with MES (Manufacturing Execution Systems) or other manufacturing systems.

Why experience is crucial

Since each implementation requires individual adjustments, experience with different JIT/JIS solutions is essential. An experienced consultant can:
Quickly analyze processes and identify the similarities and differences to other customer solutions.
Incorporate best practices from previous projects to develop an efficient and future-proof solution.
Minimize the risk of errors by identifying typical stumbling blocks (e.g. incorrect sequencing or unstable EDI connections) at an early stage.

Conclusion

JIT and JIS processes are complex, but their successful implementation ensures a significant increase in efficiency in production. The SAP standard forms a solid basis, but in practice customer-specific adaptations dominate. Here, experience counts above all - it makes the difference between a functional and a truly optimized solution.

entry.title: Error: Cannot send to the provider; please update your e-mail address and/or telephone number.
entry.content:
Unable to send the case to the service provider due to the following error:

"Cannot send to the provider; please update your e-mail address and/or telephone number. For help, see the quick guide in the Help Center. To report the incident internally, deselect the Forward to Service Provider checkbox"

"Save Failed"


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Error: Cannot send to the provider; please update you...
entry.content:
This may be because data for the key user is not maintained correctly in the system. After maintaining contact details of the key user, user can send the case to service provider.

Please maintain the following contact details for the key user in the contact data:

  • E-mail address
  • At least one phone number

Use following steps to maintain contact details for the key user:

  1. Go to the Home work center
  2. Select Self-Services view
  3. Select the Self-Services Overview sub view
  4. Select Edit My Contact Data link under the Company Address Book section
  5. Enter at least one phone number (Office Phone or Mobile Phone)
  6. Enter your E-mail address.
  7. Select Finish button to save contact details.

This can be also performed on

  1. Go to the Administrator work center
  2. Select Business Users view
  3. Select the Employee Overview sub view
  4. Search for the Employee
  5. Select Edit
  6. Add Phone and E-mail
  7. Save the changes

You can also refer to KBA: https://me.sap.com/notes/2585466

entry.title: Re: Error while adding SAP Build App
entry.content: the same here , please help

entry.title: Re: Couldn't subscribe to SAP Build Apps
entry.content: exactly the same error !!!

entry.title: Re: CIG support team does not provide support integration...
entry.content:
The SAP Managed Gateway (also known as CIG) does support Web Dispatcher, but the issues typically arise on the Web Dispatcher side. Please check the link below where I outlined some potential solutions to resolve your issue.

 

You can refer to this link: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/sap-cig-integration-with-web-dispatcher-not-working/qaq-p/12778424.

entry.title: Re: CIG support team does not provide support integration...
entry.content: I've encountered numerous issues with the Web Dispatcher with Cloud Connector, and all of them were related to Web Dispatcher configurations. In most cases, these problems were resolved by adjusting the 'wdisp/system_0' parameter and removing the 'SRCVHOST'.

entry.title: Re: I am not able to assign Charactestics to a Combinatio...
entry.content: The issue was on the authorization object, more specifically a missing activity field that needed to  be included. Problem solved

entry.title: Re: Making SAP Business One Web Client accessible externally using nginx as a reverse proxy
entry.content:
Hi  @Guy_Sujetzki 

Thanks for the blog! Everything works perfectly for the SAP Business One version on HANA.However, I cannot access the web client in the SQL version. I can successfully access SLD and authentication services, but when I try to open the Web Client, I encounter the following error:

400 Bad Request, Request Header Or Cookie Too Large, nginx.

As per the guide, there is only one change we have to when configuring for Business One SQL version,

#ssl_session_cache shared:WEB:10m;

Could you please help?

Regards,

Pradeep

entry.title: KMAT in IBP - is this possible in IBP-OBP
entry.content:
Hello experts,

is it possible to handle configurable materials (KMAT) in IBP-OBP (oder-based-planning with R&S)?

Having FERT- and KMAT-products produced at the same bottle-neck-resource.

Is it possible to make a finite-planning in IBP-OBP for both products and are operations and components of the products added in the interface back from IBP-OBP to ECC within RTI?

How are the operations and components of the KMATs getting identified.

If KMATs are not possible to handle with IBP-OBP - which other SAP-applications are recommended?

Thanks.

Jo

And the data with Travel ID 8 can only be searched by 08 or 0008 with a prefix 0, not by 8. Can anyone help explain this behavior?

 Build an SAP Fiori App Using the ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model [RAP100]

DB value:

Search by 8, no data found

Need search with prefix 0

entry.title: FIORI Elements with ODATA V2: Side Effect not triggered
entry.content:
Hi, 

I have a FIORI Elements App with an ODATA V2 Model as Backend. What I would like to achieve:

If a User enters an Input in an Field in the Object Page, an Import Function is triggered to update another Field. 

The App is non-draft. But it is not getting triggered. 

I am referring to Side Effects - Documentation - Demo Kit - SAPUI5 SDK

For my understanding: Do I have to press SAVE to trigger it? I want it to be displayed live directly after input. 

Code in BAS:

 

<Annotations Target="SAP.USER_ABC">
    <Annotation Term="com.sap.vocabularies.Common.v1.SideEffects" Qualifier="CalculatePrice">
        <Record>
            <PropertyValue Property="SourceProperties">
                <Collection>
                    <PropertyPath>CUSTOM_FIELD</PropertyPath>
                </Collection>
            </PropertyValue>
            <PropertyValue Property="TargetProperties">
                <Collection>
                    <String>CUSTOM_OUTPUT</String>
                </Collection>
            </PropertyValue>
            <PropertyValue Property="TriggerAction" String="XXX.PM_USER_SCEN_ASS_SRV.XXX_PM_USER_SCEN_ASS_SRV_Entities/CHECK"/>
        </Record>
    </Annotation>
</Annotations>

 

The Function Import is working on itself in the App.

entry.title: SAP RESTFUL ABAP custom table update along with RAP entites update not working in instance actions
entry.content:
Hi Team,

I have a instance action where I am doing postings and collecting the error logs which are read only in the Fiori. I am trying to update the status of instances using RAP EML update  and inserting the logs into custom table using normal MODIFY statement. When I use both the statements, only RAP EML update statement is working and normal ABAP MODIFY DB from internal table is not working.

ABAP EML

MODIFY ENTITIES OF zwbs_prc IN LOCAL MODE

ENTITY wbs_stg

UPDATE

FIELDS ( wbselemstatus )

WITH VALUE #( FOR ls_wbs IN rt_wbs_stg_db

( %tky = VALUE #( %key = VALUE #( prckey = ls_wbs-prc_key

zwbsuuid = ls_wbs-zwbs_uuid

)

%is_draft = if_abap_behv=>mk-off

)

wbselemstatus = ls_wbs-status

) )

REPORTED DATA(update_stg_reported).

Normal ABAP Modify   
MODIFY zwbs_err FROM TABLE lt_errors.

Can anyone help me here?

entry.title: Re: How do I make the Security Policy field a mandatory f...
entry.content: The official answer from SAP ==> 2890297 - Assigning SECPOL policies as a mandatory field for user creation/modification

entry.title: Re: Not able to transform JSON document to XML
entry.content:
The easiest is to put a content modifier (with expression) to insert the root element in the body, e.g. like this:

{
"root":
${body}
}

entry.title: Re: Bug Report – Dynamic Text Boxes Not Updating on Subpa...
entry.content: Hi William thank you for your answer, unfortunately we can not use story filter, each main page reflect different product details based on their own dates.

entry.title: Save the Date: SAP Inside Track 2025, March 12
entry.content:
We’re excited to kick off the year with SAP Inside Track Auckland 2025, and registrations are now open! This premier event is a great opportunity for SAP professionals, enthusiasts, and thought leaders to share their insights, innovations, and expertise with a dynamic community. 

Event Overview

Date: March 12, 2025 (4-7pm)
Location: Auckland
Venue:  TBA

This community-driven event is all about learning, networking, and sharing insights with fellow SAP professionals, developers, and experts. Similar to the previous events, we will be running this in the evening for #SAPCommunity to catchup and network after work. We have two impactful sessions packed lined up with real-world insights and actionable strategies. These presentations will not only showcase customer success stories but also equip you with the tools and approaches to drive a smoother, more successful transformation.
 

Agenda

On-premise to Innovation using SAP BTP
This presentation provides a comprehensive roadmap for modernizing legacy SAP landscapes using the power of SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP). We'll journey through a real-world case study on how we can expose SAP data as APIs for consumption in external channels with robust authentication. This session will also bring our learnings from SAUG-X and TechEd events. Finally, we'll explore the tangible business benefits unlocked through this BTP implementation, showcasing how this transformation enabled increased agility, reduced operational costs, and empowered the business with data-driven insights.
Speakers:  Mike Wallace & Jay Sandanam, Air New Zealand
 
Event Driven Architecture using SAP Advanced Event Mesh
Learn how real-time events and decoupled systems can drive agility, scalability, and digital transformation in your organization.
Speaker: Advit Ramesh, Accenture

How to Register

We have limited spots. Register here for free and join us on March 12th. 

We look forward to seeing you. Do subscribe to the Auckland Group to receive notifications of similar events.

entry.title: Inventory items with zero Cost of Goods Sold and it needs recalculation
entry.content:
The Cost of Goods Sold is not been impacted because of missing inventory cost of some products being sold.
Apparently, the system posts zero cost against the affected products in Goods and Activity Confirmation journal posted prior to the customer invoice.

You want to know if there is a way in the system to go back and recalculate the COGS that was zero so that it can be corrected?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Inventory items with zero Cost of Goods Sold and it n...
entry.content:
To recalculate the Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) in SAP Business by Design, you need to access the "Inventory Valuation" work center and utilize the "Actual Cost Allocation" functionality, which allows you to re-evaluate inventory and COGS by posting the difference between preliminary postings and calculated actual costs based on your chosen costing method.

For more details, you may refer to help documents Actual Cost Allocation Runs

entry.title: Groovy and learning basics
entry.content:
Hi Experts, I am new to groovy and learning basics. I need know the documentation where I can know the methods that I can use in Groovy for CPÎ? and the libraries used.

For example .

getHeader

getProperties, etc

entry.title: Re: SItuation Scenarios
entry.content:
Hi @venkatesh007, 

There could be several reasons why you're not receiving notifications about a situation.

Firstly, were you able to generate situation instances? Secondly, how did you configure the recipients in your situation type? You'll only be notified if your user is identified as a recipient for situation notifications. For example, if you're using a team for agent determination, your user needs to be added as a team member.

For a more detailed investigation into the topic of missing notifications, please refer to the following blog post: Help – Why don't I get a situation notification?

Kind regards,

Ana

entry.title: Is it mandatory to have a partner for a lift and shift to RISE
entry.content:
Dear members,

While going through SAP documentation about  " Implementing SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition " , it talks about working with SAP Partner in various stages .  

We have a full IT team with in the organization and we do very limited work with partners . Is it mandatory to work with a partner for RISE implementation ?  I am specifically interested to know this answer in the context of RISE lift and shift option. 

Regards

Krishna

entry.title: Re: How to Get custom CDS view in Custom field in F4 value help
entry.content: Hi gunadharshini, I have informed the responsible expert on your question. At the same time, could you please add "SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Asset Management" tag and remove general one? Regards, Irene

entry.title: Enhancing SAP TPM promotion updates with robust ABAP coding for a more efficient and streamlined day
entry.content:
Hi Everyone,

Happy weekend to all!

Recently, I encountered a scenario where I frequently performed a task manually. To streamline the process and save time, I developed an automation code that makes it much easier. As a family member I would like to share this new learning with you all. 

As an SAP BW-IP-TPM developer, this is one of the most common activities we handle regularly while working on Trade Promotion Management (TPM) in SAP.

The goal of this automation is to update the SAP TPM promotion status in the backend CRM system efficiently.

Thank me Later 🙂

Kishore
SAP BW-IP-TPM Developer

 

TABLES: crm_jest.

* Selection Screen Parameters
PARAMETERS: p_guid   TYPE char32 OBLIGATORY,  " GUID from user
            p_ostat  TYPE char8,           " Current Status (old)
            p_nstat  TYPE char8,            " New Status (new)
            p_active AS CHECKBOX,             " Checkbox to activate status
            p_insert AS CHECKBOX.             " Checkbox to insert new record

DATA: ls_jest TYPE crm_jest.

START-OF-SELECTION.

  " =========================== STEP 1: UPDATE STATUS (IF NEEDED) ===========================
  IF p_ostat IS NOT INITIAL AND p_nstat IS NOT INITIAL.
    " Fetch current status
    SELECT SINGLE * FROM crm_jest
    WHERE objnr = p_guid
      AND stat = p_ostat.

    IF sy-subrc = 0.
      " Update status from OLD to NEW
      UPDATE crm_jest
        SET stat = p_nstat
      WHERE objnr = p_guid
        AND stat = p_ostat.

      COMMIT WORK.
      WRITE: / 'Status changed from', p_ostat, 'to', p_nstat, 'for GUID:', p_guid.
    ELSE.
      WRITE: / 'No matching status', p_ostat, 'found for GUID:', p_guid.
    ENDIF.
  ENDIF.

  " =========================== STEP 2: CHANGE INACTIVE TO ACTIVE ===========================
  IF p_active = 'X'.
    " If the user selected 'Active', remove 'X'
    SELECT SINGLE * FROM crm_jest
    WHERE objnr = p_guid
      AND stat = p_nstat
      AND inact = 'X'.

    IF sy-subrc = 0.
      " Change status from Inactive (X) to Active (blank)
      UPDATE crm_jest
        SET inact = ''
      WHERE objnr = p_guid
        AND stat = p_nstat
        AND inact = 'X'.

      COMMIT WORK.
      WRITE: / 'Status', p_nstat, 'is now active for GUID:', p_guid.
    ELSE.
      WRITE: / 'No inactive status found for GUID:', p_guid.
    ENDIF.

  ELSE. " IF p_active is NOT selected, then make it inactive (X)

    SELECT SINGLE * FROM crm_jest
    WHERE objnr = p_guid
      AND stat = p_nstat
      AND inact = ''.

    IF sy-subrc = 0.
      " Change status from Active (blank) to Inactive (X)
      UPDATE crm_jest
        SET inact = 'X'
      WHERE objnr = p_guid
        AND stat = p_nstat
        AND inact = ''.

      COMMIT WORK.
      WRITE: / 'Status', p_nstat, 'is now inactive (X) for GUID:', p_guid.
    ELSE.
      WRITE: / 'No active status found for GUID:', p_guid.
    ENDIF.

  ENDIF.

 

 #SAPTPM #TPM #SAP #SAPBW #SAPIP #SAPBWIPTPM #TradePromotionManagement CRM Analytics RISE with SAP

entry.title: Re: Use groovy script to extract attachment content from soap message in CPI
entry.content:
Hi,

Receiving this error when trying to implement the scenario above.

 
Error Details
nbound processing in endpoint at /readAttachment failed with message "SoapFault:faultstring=Error reading XMLStreamReader: Unexpected character '-' (code 45) in prolog; expected '<' at [row,col {unknown-source}]: [1,1]; faultcode={http://www.w3.org/2003/05/soap-envelope}Sender", caused by "WstxUnexpectedCharException:Unexpected character '-' (code 45) in prolog; expected '<' at [row,col {unknown-source}]: [1,1]"
 
Is there any change required to the code ?
Not sure what I am missing.
 
Thanks, 
SB

Cust. Object V_TCURV is not mapped in WEBGUIregistry to the IMG activity SIMG_CFMENUCX01_CU05

 

Can you please help me to resolve this issue.

 

Thanks & Regards,

entry.title: Re: Use groovy script to extract attachment content from soap message in CPI

Hi, 

I realized that the issue is because of the xml file that I was attaching in the SOAP Call.  When I attached another a different xml file it worked fine.  Your scenario worked great.

Thx / SB

entry.title: Change report painter period parameter transferred to RKPEP000
entry.content:
Hi,

I am facing a problem about report painter and called up report RKPEP000, if you have any idea about this case , could you please advise? Thanks.

The report painter report is created in group 6P3 which is related to WBS element. When I executed the report, it shows the first screen and then I double click the line at the screen and the second screen displays(The second screen is the called up report RKPEP000).

At the first screen, a column is "balance by the first-half of current year", it shows the data of below two parts:

For example, current year is 2024, the first part is "period 1~12, year 1900~2023" and the second part is "period 1~6, year 2024". There is no problem at the first screen, but at the second screen, the report shows data of "period 1~12, year 1900~2024", the period 7~12 year 2024's data should not be showed.

The reason is that the parameter transferred to the second screen is combined, so the period is 1~12.

Is there any method can solve this problem? I want to show the correct data at the second screen.

entry.title: Re: The use of Class CL_APS_BC_SCOPE_CHANGE_API is not pe...
entry.content: The issue while activating the mentioned class for calling the scoping API is fixed by update the ADT version. But the issue of access Managae Launchpad Pages app ( app ID F4512, Application Component CA-FLP-FE-DT ) still persists. Can anybody from the component\app owner team in SAP provide a solution for this? Thanks much.

entry.title: Re: Learning RAP100 tutorials with permission related issues
entry.content: I encountered the same issue.

entry.title: Organizational Unit error for business partner
entry.content:
Hi all, I am using the SAP Practice for Implementing SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, Transportation Management. I hit a problem with creating (saving) a freight agreement in exercise 4. The error is:

Organizational Unit 1010: No data maintained for business partner CAR-020

I have created the carrier and it is indeed in the system so I am not sure why I am getting this issue. Is this the right channel for me to report issue?

Cheers

SFC

entry.title: Service Confirmation is cancelled but the status remains Not Started
entry.content:
The service confirmation lines are all cancelled, but the service confirmation status remains Not Started instead of Cancelled.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Service Confirmation is cancelled but the status rema...
entry.content:
The service confirmation is cancelled before released, so the document and item status remain Not Started, even though the document is cancelled.

In the service confirmations view, open the advanced search. Set the cancellation status=cancelled and search the confirmation, you can see the confirmation is filtered out, this means that the confirmation is cancelled. There are several status on a service confirmation: status; cancellation status; approval status; delivery status and invoice status. 

To confirm the cancellation status, you will need to check the cancellation status.

This is the correct system designed behavior.

entry.title: Re: Cannot able to combine the individual production orde...
entry.content: Hi， I also encountered the same problem, and later found that the header unit of the process route for obtaining production orders was different from the basic unit of measurement.

entry.title: Re: Fiori Elements ListReport with OData V4 - Cannot hide...
entry.content:
Hi,

This is by design and can be seen in the note in https://sapui5.hana.ondemand.com/#/topic/c0f6592a592e47f9bb6d09900de47412:

"If the path-based value for UI.Hidden evaluates to true for all rows, then only the fields are hidden and not the entire column."

The reasoning is that if the entire column were to be hidden, it causes confusion to end user when dealing with variants. For example, if currently all the rows have no values and the column is hidden and if user saves this as a variant - what should happen when this variant is restored in a context where some of the rows have a value for this column? Suddenly bringing back the column might confuse the user who saw this variant w/o the column earlier!

Best Regards,

   Sandeep

entry.title: Re: Use group separator for barcode in Adobe Forms
entry.content: Can you explain how you did that by using Smartforms?

entry.title: Monitor/Manage applications in S4 HANA disappear intermittently
entry.content:
We have intermittent issues when apps intermittently disappear from the launchpad. 

No business role assignments were changed and clearing the cache sometimes works or waiting till the next day "fixes" the issue.

entry.title: Re: SAP Approuter - User API Service
entry.content: Hi @sai_pavan98, may I check whether you have a solution for this issue already. I am facing the same issue.

entry.title: How to restrict authorization for accounting document reversal (FB08) and reset cleared item (FBRA)
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

We are writing to request detailed guidance on how to control authorization for accounting document reversal (FB08) and reset cleared item (FBRA) in our SAP Public Cloud environment.

Currently, we have to restrict these authorizations to only 1-2 members of our Customer accounting team. In SAP on-premise version, we controlled these authorizations through T-code (FB08, FBRA) access. However, we have not been able to locate the corresponding apps in the Public Cloud environment.

 

We would appreciate it if you could provide us with detailed instructions on how to achieve this restriction in the Public Clou

entry.title: Re: Out Now: Free Tier Model for SAP Master Data Governance, Cloud Edition
entry.content: i can't find the mdg in SERVICE MARKPLACE for my free trial,Can anybody help me with that issue ?

entry.title: Re: Try out SAP Master Data Governance, cloud edition for free!
Question about table of background job
entry.content:
Hi everyone

Please teach me the table name that I can confirm the Job frequency of a background job in list

Thank you so much!

Hana7

entry.title: Re: /SCWM/TO_CREATE_WHR Create Putaway Warehouse Task for...
entry.content:
<ls_create_whr_putaway>-rdoccat "(Inbound Delivery Doc Cat)
<ls_create_whr_putaway>-rdocid  "(Inbound Delivery Doc ID)
<ls_create_whr_putaway>-ritmid  "(Inbound Delivery Item ID)
I got the answer!

<ls_create_whr_putaway>-nlpla   "(Target S.Bin)
<ls_create_whr_putaway>-nltyp   "(Target S.Type)
<ls_create_whr_putaway>-nlber  ​ "(Target Storage Section)
<ls_create_whr_putaway>-nlner  ​ "(Target HU)

<ls_create_whr_putaway>-vlner  ​ "(Source HU)

<ls_create_whr_putaway>-anfme   "(Quantity of HU)
<ls_create_whr_putaway>-altme   "(Quantity UOM)

CALL FUNCTION '/SCWM/TO_CREATE_WHR'
  EXPORTING
    iv_lgnum       = '0100'
    iv_process     = wmegc_whr_proc_pu
    iv_mve_hu      = 'X'
    iv_to_init     = 'X'
    iv_bname       = sy-uname
    it_create_whr  = lt_create_putaway_single
    iv_update_task = ' '
    iv_commit_work = abap_true
  IMPORTING
    ev_tanum       = lv_wt_result
    et_ltap_vb     = lt_ltap_vb
    et_bapiret     = lt_bapiret
    ev_severity    = lv_severity.

entry.title: Re: How to Reduce Import Time for 1M Records in SAP Custo...
entry.content:
Hi @Rvanjari 

I'm not sure from which systems and by which methods like API or file you're importing the user accounts to CDC, could you also check the other system and make sure the data extraction and transmission performance of the source system is good.
On the CDC side, you may try splitting the 1M user records to multiple batches and running multiple dataflows in parallel, it might improve the performance.
